assertionResults.reason: Direct navigation to #node-pe-terminal-beta did restore the intended node and Product Evolution branch, and the same node reopened after refresh, but both the initial direct load and refresh triggered a visible React hydration mismatch/runtime issue in the page. The lower-left Next.js issue badge remained visible after the deep-link flow, so the deep link does not stay cleanly correct after refresh.

observedState: URL stayed at /#node-pe-terminal-beta before and after refresh.
The detail panel for 'BETTER Terminal (Closed Beta)' was visible after direct load and again after refresh.
Runtime error text reported a hydration mismatch where the server rendered Product Evolution collapsed but the client reopened it from the hash state.
evidence.consoleErrors: Hydration failed because the server rendered HTML didn't match the client... aria-expanded was rendered false on the server and true on the client for the Product Evolution branch during the deep-link load/refresh flow.

assertionResults.reason: Opening /#node-nonexistent-id showed a coherent fallback banner with explanatory copy and a visible 'Reset & Explore Roadmap' recovery action instead of a blank or broken atlas. Activating the recovery action cleared the stale hash, returned the user to the normal roadmap overview, and kept the interface usable.

observedFallback: Fallback copy stated that the linked roadmap node was not found and may have been renamed or removed.
The recovery button reset the URL from /#node-nonexistent-id to / and left the roadmap explorer in its normal overview state.
evidence.networkErrors: none observed; agent-browser request log captured no failing requests for the stale-hash fallback flow.

assertionResults.reason: The roadmap controls were operable by keyboard: focusing the Product Evolution branch toggle allowed Space/Enter activation, Tab moved into node buttons, Enter opened the selected node detail, and Escape closed the detail panel. The branch toggle, node button, detail region, and close control also exposed matching accessible names and state attributes.

observedSemantics: Product Evolution branch toggle exposed role=button, accessible name 'Product Evolution', and aria-expanded=true after keyboard expansion.
The first roadmap node exposed role=button, accessible name 'BETTER Terminal (Closed Beta)', and aria-pressed=true when selected.
The open detail surface exposed role=region with accessible name 'Details for BETTER Terminal (Closed Beta)', and the dismiss control exposed accessible name 'Close detail panel'.

assertionResults.reason: On an iPhone 14-sized viewport, roadmap branch labels and the BETTER Terminal detail text stayed readable, the Product Evolution branch and child node remained tappable, the selected node showed an active pressed state, and vertical scrolling still worked after opening the detail panel instead of fighting the page.

observedMobileState: The mobile roadmap showed full branch labels for Product Evolution, Token Utility & Access Tiers, Revenue Expansion, Technical Infrastructure, and Social & Agent Ecosystem without truncated touch targets in the tested viewport.
Tapping Product Evolution exposed child nodes, and tapping BETTER Terminal opened a readable detail card with the node marked pressed/active.
Scrolling downward after opening the detail card remained possible in the same narrow-screen flow.

frictions: Because the session had to remain single-browser per instructions, the visible Next.js issue badge raised by the VAL-ROADMAP-006 hydration mismatch stayed present in later screenshots even after later flows reported no new browser errors.

Hash-only fallback navigation did not produce a captured request log in agent-browser, so VAL-ROADMAP-007 network evidence is recorded as no observed failing requests rather than a detailed request trace.

summary.note: Three assigned assertions passed (007, 008, 009). VAL-ROADMAP-006 failed because the valid deep-link flow restored the intended node but consistently raised a hydration mismatch/runtime issue on direct load and refresh.
